task_completion.description: Search for patents related to wireless power transfer or inductive charging on Google Patents or USPTO.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser open to patents.google.com or uspto.gov with relevant search terms (e.g., 'wireless power transfer', 'inductive charging') in the search bar and search results displayed.

task_completion.description: Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Patent Landscape Research'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox bookmark toolbar, sidebar, or library window showing a folder explicitly named 'Patent Landscape Research'.

task_completion.description: Bookmark at least 5 specific patent pages in the 'Patent Landscape Research' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Patent Landscape Research' bookmark folder is shown expanded or open, containing at least 5 distinct links/entries to individual patent pages.

task_completion.description: Create a JSON report at ~/Documents/patent_landscape.json with the required top-level structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying a file named 'patent_landscape.json' located in '~/Documents/' containing the top-level JSON keys: "search_topic", "patents", "key_assignees", and "summary".

task_completion.description: Include at least 5 patents in the JSON report with all required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The "patents" array in the JSON file contains at least 5 objects, and each object includes the keys: "patent_number", "title", "assignee", "grant_date", and "cpc_code".

task_completion.description: Ensure patent numbers are valid US granted patents and dates use YYYY-MM-DD format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the JSON file, patent numbers start with 'US' followed by digits (and typically a suffix like B2), and all "grant_date" values strictly follow the YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g., '2016-05-31').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Firefox GUI to search and bookmark patents.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show actual interaction with the Firefox browser interface (clicking, typing in search bars, using the bookmark menu) rather than bypassing the UI with terminal scripts like curl or sqlite3.

integrity.description: Verify the patents listed in the JSON report match the ones bookmarked and viewed in the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patent numbers and titles entered into the JSON file should correspond to the patents seen in the browser tabs or bookmark list during the research phase.

integrity.description: Verify the JSON file was manually created and populated or generated through legitimate scripting by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing or pasting the gathered data into a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code) or writing a script to format the JSON, rather than the file magically appearing fully formed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Valid US utility patent numbers start with 'US' followed by 6-11 digits and typically a letter suffix (e.g., US8193764B2). Grant dates must strictly follow the YYYY-MM-DD format (ISO 8601). The task requires finding real patents using genuine patent databases like patents.google.com, uspto.gov, or espacenet.com. The keywords 'wireless', 'power', 'charging', 'inductive', 'magnetic', 'coupling', and 'transfer' are highly relevant to this domain.
